skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides agents through the final steps for a completed development branch, including test verification, merge options, pull request creation, and safe cleanup.
skill.summary: Finish completed development branches with tests, merge or PR choices, and controlled cleanup.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command examples and two false-positive weak crypto detections. Review found legitimate Git and test workflow commands, but the skill can change repository state, push branches, create pull requests, and delete branches or worktrees after confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Modifying Shell Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Git and project test commands that can switch branches, pull changes, merge a branch, push to origin, create a pull request, delete a feature branch, and remove a worktree. These actions match the skill purpose and include a typed confirmation before forced deletion, but they can alter local and remote repository state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands are explicit and semantically aligned with branch completion. Risk is from legitimate repository mutation and deletion, not from evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in Examples
low_findings.description: The pull request command uses command substitution to pass a heredoc body to the GitHub CLI, and the worktree check substitutes the current branch name into grep. These are common shell patterns, but unreviewed placeholder replacement or unusual branch names could cause incorrect command behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitutions are visible in shell examples and appear operational rather than malicious. The concern is limited to robustness and safe quoting.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at the metadata description line, but that line only describes development workflow completion. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive hashing logic is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains descriptive prose only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Standard Device File Redirect
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged access to /dev/null in the merge-base command. The redirect only suppresses command error output while checking common base branches and does not read or modify user files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The /dev/null references are standard shell redirection in a Git branch detection command. No evidence found of sensitive filesystem access or persistence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Branch and Worktree Deletion Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands to force-delete a feature branch and remove a worktree after typed discard confirmation. This is not malicious, but it is destructive if applied to the wrong branch or path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive commands and confirmation text are directly present in the skill. The surrounding instructions reduce accidental execution risk but do not remove the destructive impact.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Repository Publishing Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a push command and a GitHub CLI pull request creation command. These commands are expected for the workflow, but they can publish branch contents and metadata to a remote service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote publishing commands are explicit. No evidence found of credential theft or hidden exfiltration, but repository content can be intentionally published.

content.value_statement: Completed code still needs a clean path into the main workflow. This skill guides test checks, merge or pull request choices, and branch cleanup with confirmation.
actual_capabilities: Prompts the agent to verify the project test suite before any merge or pull request action.
Helps identify a likely base branch with git merge-base checks for main or master.
Presents four completion options: local merge, pull request, keep branch, or discard work.
Provides command sequences for local merge, branch push, GitHub pull request creation, and cleanup.
Requires typed confirmation before discarding work and deleting a feature branch.
Defines when to preserve or remove a Git worktree after completion.
limitations: It assumes the project has a known test command and Git repository workflow.
It does not inspect repository policies, required reviews, or protected branch settings.
It relies on the agent to replace placeholders with correct branch names and paths.
It can guide destructive cleanup, so users must review branch and worktree details first.

use_cases.title: Complete a feature branch after tests pass
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose whether to merge locally, create a pull request, or keep the branch for later review.

use_cases.title: Standardize handoff from AI coding work
use_cases.description: Use the fixed option list to make AI-generated implementation work easier to review and integrate.

use_cases.title: Clean up worktrees after branch completion
use_cases.description: Use the cleanup rules to remove worktrees only when the selected completion path permits it.

prompt_templates.title: Finish This Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to finish my current development branch. First verify tests, then present the available completion options.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to verify the branch and guide me through pushing it and creating a pull request with a clear summary and test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Locally With Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Integrate a finished branch into the base branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to finish the branch by checking tests, confirming the base branch, merging locally, retesting, and cleaning up only when safe.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Completion Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow decision with safety checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit the current branch state, identify the likely base branch, explain any blocked conditions, and execute only the completion path I approve.

output_examples.input: The implementation is done and tests should be passing.
output_examples.output: The agent verifies the test suite, reports the result, and offers four choices: merge locally, create a pull request, keep the branch, or discard it.

output_examples.input: Create a pull request for this completed branch.
output_examples.output: The agent checks tests first, pushes the branch when approved, creates a pull request draft with summary and test plan sections, and preserves the worktree.
The agent lists what will be deleted and waits for the exact confirmation word before deleting the branch and removing the worktree.

best_practices: Run the complete project test suite before choosing any integration path.
Review the detected base branch, feature branch, and worktree path before executing commands.
Use the discard option only after confirming that commits and worktree contents are no longer needed.
anti_patterns: Skipping test verification because the implementation appears complete.
Deleting a branch or worktree without reading the confirmation details.
Creating a pull request with placeholder titles, summaries, or test steps still present.

faq.question: What does this skill help complete?
faq.answer: It helps complete a development branch after implementation work is done and tests are ready to verify.

faq.question: Does it automatically merge code?
faq.answer: No. It presents options and should execute a merge only after the user chooses that path.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a GitHub CLI workflow for pushing the branch and creating a pull request.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains legitimate commands that can change repository state, publish branches, or delete local work.

faq.question: Does it protect against accidental discard?
faq.answer: It requires typed confirmation before branch deletion, but users still need to review the listed commits and path.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
